name: claude-hooks-stop-vs-sessionend
tags: claude-hooks, stop, sessionend, lifecycle, per-turn, UPS, checkpoint, trim, evict, reliable
body:
Stop hook fires at the end of EVERY assistant response — not at session end. Never do session-scoped cleanup on Stop — it wipes cross-turn state every turn.

SessionEnd and SessionStart are unreliable — not guaranteed to fire on crashes, force-quits, or reconnects. Do NOT rely on them for critical logic.

**Correct wiring:**
- Stop → finalise turn only (no eviction)
- SessionEnd → unreliable, do not depend on it
- UPS → checkpoint trim runs here (5-session cap enforced on every prompt, raised from 2 on 2026-07-05 — task:d2c347ca)

**Why:** Checkpoint hygiene was moved from SessionEnd to UPS precisely because SessionEnd cannot be trusted to always fire.
